name: GAX positioning vs benchmarks
summary: Benchmarks support GAX as a third surface: CLI-shaped invocations, MCP-class governance, optimized-MCP-class lazy discovery. Naive MCP loses on tokens (4x-32x) and reliability (28% timeouts in Scalekit). Optimized MCP (programmatic, Code Mode, gateway filter) closes the token gap but does not standardize caps, audit_id per invoke, or shell ergonomics in one protocol. Repo eval (eval/run_comparison.py) ranks gax composite ~0.99 vs cli ~0.68 vs mcp_naive_43 ~0.56 on weighted tokens/reliability/governance/structure.
methodology_notes: Synthesis across Phase 1 sources and local eval harness (token heuristic chars/4, MCP schema from fixtures). Live MCP in eval optional via --live-mcp. Phase 3: publish with provider token APIs.
implications_for_gax: Continue: MCP bridge implementation, OpenAPI manifest generator, Phase 3 vault/SPIFFE. Message for stakeholders: GAX is best on composite agent metrics; CLI remains valid for ungoverned local speed; naive MCP is not cost-competitive.
corrections_to_repo_docs: research/10-evaluation: link comparison.json after each eval run.
uncertain: eval uses simulated MCP schema tax for most runs
